clusters.title: Penny Wong gives emotional speech as Pauline Hanson is censured in Australian Senate – video
clusters.neutral_headline: Pauline Hanson Censured In Australian Senate

items.title: Penny Wong gives emotional speech as Pauline Hanson is censured in Australian Senate – video
items.standfirst: Senator Pauline Hanson has faced a censure motion over her comments to Sky News in February, where the One Nation leader said: 'You say, “Well, there’s good Muslims out there.” How can you tell me there are good Muslims?' The leader of the government in the Senate, Penny Wong, said that ‘to claim that “none of them are good” is to say there are no good Australians amongst them; is to tell a child that no matter how hard they study, how kindly they treat others, how much they love this country, they will never belong.’ The Senate has voted 36 to 17 to censure Pauline Hanson over her comments about Muslim Australians.Australian politics updatesPauline Hanson expected to face censure motion in Senate over ‘inflammatory’ comments about Muslims Continue reading...
items.content: Senator Pauline Hanson has faced a censure motion over her comments to Sky News in February, where the One Nation leader said: 'You say, “Well, there’s good Muslims out there.” How can you tell me there are good Muslims?' The leader of the government in the Senate, Penny Wong, said that ‘to claim that “none of them are good” is to say there are no good Australians amongst them; is to tell a child that no matter how hard they study, how kindly they treat others, how much they love this country, they will never belong.’ The Senate has voted 36 to 17 to censure Pauline Hanson over her comments about Muslim Australians.Australian politics updatesPauline Hanson expected to face censure motion in Senate over ‘inflammatory’ comments about Muslims Continue reading...

items.title: Hanson censured over no 'good' Muslims comment, Coalition votes against
items.standfirst: Two Liberal senators cross the floor to vote for Labor's motion that censured the One Nation leader for "inflammatory and divisive comments" that sought to vilify Muslim Australians.
items.content: Two Liberal senators cross the floor to vote for Labor's motion that censured the One Nation leader for "inflammatory and divisive comments" that sought to vilify Muslim Australians.
